scope_note: This pair covers ONLY the EXOGENOUS (landscape-originated) wildfire physical-damage slice: fire ignites outside the site in vegetation, spreads, reaches the asset, and damages it via flame/radiant/ember/convective heat. Magnitude axis is FSim conditional flame-length -> fireline intensity, NOT ecological burn severity (dNBR/MTBS = geography check only). Explicitly OUT OF SCOPE (named-and-deferred distinct perils): endogenous/asset-originated fire (inverter, connector/wiring, combiner, module/junction-box, transformer, BESS thermal runaway), smoke/soiling optical generation loss, PSPS, business interruption.
golden_rule_on_origin: A source with a fire number is NOT an exogenous-wildfire benchmark until its ignition-origin frame (external landscape vs equipment-origin vs blended), denominator, coverage, asset class, geography, and loss basis are understood. Every fire number carries a fire_origin_family tag so wrong-origin numbers are quarantined, not silently averaged.

upstream_cell_governance.hazard_axis: FSIM_CONDITIONAL_FLAME_LENGTH_PROBABILITY (USFS FSim, 270 m; 6 conditional flame-length bins)

notes: solar_wildfire crosswalk is RESOLVED_GRAIN_DR_WITHHELD: the canonical wildfire_solar cell exists so the 11 named WS_* failure units and their value shares are used verbatim, but the cell is a v0.1 scaffold with ordinate_status=withheld / withheld_reason_codes=[NO_RUNTIME_CURVE], so NO damage ratio is available and every DR ordinate cell is blank/WITHHELD. This is neither PENDING (the cell exists) nor curve-resolved (DR withheld).

The wildfire DIRECT failure grain spans nearly every subsystem via thermal attack (11 WS_* units), unlike hail (one module-glass unit) or flood (~8 mostly-electrical units). DIRECT-hardware envelope (rows 2-10) = $656,981/MWdc = 58.66% installed TIV; PV module hardware cap = $291,215/MWdc = 26.00%. Value ladder reused from solar_hail/solar_flood because it is peril-agnostic.

0 of 100 benchmark rows normalize to a $/MW basis. Every normalized_usd_per_MW cell is blank (never zero). Isolated, properly-scoped EXOGENOUS-wildfire solar $/MW physical severity is genuinely absent from the open literature.

The largest open fire families are the WRONG origin/peril for this cell: ALL_CAUSE_PV_FIRE_BLENDED (kWh/GCube/AXIS/Marsh) blends endogenous+exogenous ignition and is tagged CONTEXT_NOT_BENCHMARK; SMOKE_SOILING_GENERATION is a separate revenue peril. kWh's '84% equipment-driven' headline is unsupported by its own chart (84% = 100% - 16% wildfire, sweeping 27% unknown + 3% other into 'equipment').

Named 'solar fire' events were origin-checked: DEPCOM Kern, Wellington North, Beryl, Canyon Fire 2, Kings County, Energy Safe Vic = EXOGENOUS; CVSR 2019 (avian/electrical arc), Mannum & Raywood (inverter) = ENDOGENOUS and excluded from the exogenous count.

The wildfire_solar curve (once promoted) will be a public-source-anchored engineering parameterization on an FSim conditional flame-length axis; the cell owns severity curves only (EAL/PML/VaR/TVaR are downstream in the hazard catalog / financial model).
Row counts above equal the actual data-row counts of the corresponding CSVs (benchmark=100, source_matrix=63, crosswalk=3, intensity=6) and the source_registry.json length (63).
